_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 4-4 Pediatric Altered Mental Status (Revised 03/17/2025, MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan Obstetrics and Pediatrics Protocol Section 4-4: Pediatric Altered Mental Status — MDHHS Approved 3/17/25, Reviewed 2025
purpose: Assessment and treatment of pediatric patients with altered mental status of unknown etiology such as alcohol, trauma, poisonings, seizures, behavioral problems, stroke, environmental causes, infection, etc. For pediatrics < 24 hours old: refer to Newborn/Neonatal Assessment and Resuscitation-Treatment Protocol. For critically ill patients: refer to Pediatric Crashing Patient/Impending Arrest-Treatment Protocol.

sections.title: Pediatric Altered Mental Status Treatment
points: Pediatric patients (< 14 years) utilize MI-MEDIC for appropriate medication dosage — when unavailable utilize pediatric dosing listed within protocol
Restrain patient if necessary — refer to Patient Restraint-Procedure Protocol
Ensure adequate oxygenation, ventilation, and work of breathing: monitor SpO2; consider use of capnography (Specialist skill)
Check blood glucose (may be MFR skill — see Blood Glucose Testing-Procedure Protocol)
Check temperature — if febrile, go to Pediatric Fever-Treatment Protocol
Start IV/IO if needed per Vascular Access & IV Therapy-Procedure Protocol (Specialist skill)

If blood glucose < 60 mg/dL: if altered but able to swallow AND 3 months old or older — administer oral glucose; if not alert — administer dextrose per MI-MEDIC or weight-based dosing table (Specialist skill): Grey (0–2 mo, 3–5 kg) 2.5g Dextrose 12.5% 20 mL OR D10% 25 mL; Pink (3–6 mo, 6–7 kg) 3.25g D25% 13 mL OR D10% 33 mL; Red (7–10 mo, 8–9 kg) 4.25g D25% 17 mL OR D10% 43 mL; Purple (11–18 mo, 10–11 kg) 5g D25% 20 mL OR D10% 50 mL; Yellow (19–35 mo, 12–14 kg) 6.25g D25% 25 mL OR D10% 63 mL; White (3–4 yr, 15–18 kg) 8g D25% 32 mL OR D10% 80 mL; Blue (5–6 yr, 19–23 kg) 10g D25% 40 mL OR D10% 100 mL; Orange (7–9 yr, 24–29 kg) 12.5g D50% 25 mL OR D10% 125 mL; Green (10–14 yr, 30–36 kg) 15g D50% 40 mL OR D10% 150 mL

Per MCA selection, if unable to start IV: administer glucagon IM/IN (if available per MCA selection) per MI-MEDIC — may be EMT skill per MCA selection; if MI-MEDIC unavailable: patients < 5 years administer glucagon 0.5 mg IM; patients ≥ 5 years administer glucagon 1 mg IM; for IN (Baqsimi only): patients < 5 years — DO NOT ADMINISTER; patients ≥ 5 years administer 3 mg IN

If patient respiratory depression persists and/or patient has not regained consciousness despite adequate oxygenation and ventilatory support: administer naloxone per Opioid Overdose Treatment and Prevention-Treatment Protocol
Contact Medical Control for repeat dextrose
Contact Medical Control for repeat naloxone

NOTE: To obtain D10%: discard 40 mL from D50 amp, draw up 40 mL NS; to obtain D12.5%: discard 37.5 mL from D50 amp, draw up 37.5 mL NS; to obtain D25%: discard 25 mL from D50 amp, draw up 25 mL NS; may utilize D10% for all ages 5 mL/kg (0.5 gm/kg) up to 250 mL; dextrose should always be pushed slowly (over 1–2 minutes)

mca_selections_required.description: MCA may designate blood glucose testing as an MFR skill
MCA must select which provider levels may administer glucagon IM (injectable formulation only): < 5 years = 0.5 mg IM; ≥ 5 years = 1 mg IM
MCA must select which provider levels may administer glucagon 3 mg IN (FDA-approved IN formulation only — DO NOT administer to patients < 5 years of age)

key_drugs.name: Dextrose IV (Pediatric — Weight-Based)
key_drugs.dose: Per MI-MEDIC cards; backup table by color zone: Grey 2.5g, Pink 3.25g, Red 4.25g, Purple 5g, Yellow 6.25g, White 8g, Blue 10g, Orange 12.5g, Green 15g. May use D10% 5 mL/kg (0.5 gm/kg) for all ages up to 250 mL.
key_drugs.route: IV (patent IV required — to avoid extravasation)
key_drugs.indication: Blood glucose < 60 mg/dL and patient not alert
key_drugs.contraindications: Concentrated solutions (D50) avoided in small children — dilute to appropriate concentration per table
key_drugs.side_effects: Hyperglycemia, phlebitis, tissue necrosis with extravasation
key_drugs.notes: Always push slowly over 1–2 minutes. Contact Medical Control for repeat doses. Use MI-MEDIC first; use weight/color table when MI-MEDIC unavailable.

key_drugs.name: Glucagon IM (Pediatric)
key_drugs.dose: Patients < 5 years: 0.5 mg IM; patients ≥ 5 years: 1 mg IM (injectable formulation ONLY)
key_drugs.indication: Hypoglycemia when IV access cannot be established
key_drugs.contraindications: Injectable formulation must NOT be given intranasally; pheochromocytoma
key_drugs.scope: Per MCA selection — EMT through Paramedic options available
key_drugs.notes: Do NOT use injectable glucagon via IN route. Use MI-MEDIC when available.

key_drugs.name: Glucagon IN — Baqsimi (Pediatric)
key_drugs.dose: Patients < 5 years: DO NOT ADMINISTER; patients ≥ 5 years: 3 mg IN
key_drugs.route: Intranasal (FDA-approved IN formulation ONLY — e.g., Baqsimi)
key_drugs.indication: Hypoglycemia ≥ 5 years when IV access cannot be established
key_drugs.contraindications: Age < 5 years — DO NOT ADMINISTER; only FDA-approved IN formulation may be used
key_drugs.scope: Per MCA selection — EMT through Paramedic options available
key_drugs.notes: EMS clinicians may assist family/caregivers in administering FDA-approved IN glucagon if prescribed for the patient (regardless of age).

deterioration_flags: Blood glucose < 60 mg/dL, not alert — IV dextrose per weight table (Specialist skill); glucagon IM/IN if no IV (per MCA selection)
Persistent respiratory depression despite adequate support — naloxone per Opioid Overdose protocol
Unable to regain consciousness despite glucose correction — contact Medical Control
High glucose, febrile — check temperature; refer to Pediatric Fever protocol; consider sepsis
scope_notes: Airway assessment, SpO2 monitoring, oral glucose (alert patients ≥ 3 months): all levels
Blood glucose testing: all levels — may be MFR skill per MCA selection
Dextrose IV: Specialist skill — use MI-MEDIC or weight table; always dilute appropriately
Glucagon IM/IN: per MCA selection — age-specific dosing; IN contraindicated < 5 years
